description:
You are the configuration-management engineer for a small fleet of servers.  
  Two source data files have already been placed for you on disk.  
  Your goal is to use classic Unix **cut(1)** and **paste(1)** style column-manipulation techniques
  to build a consolidated change-tracking matrix and a small verification log.

  1.  Source files (they already exist):
      a.  /home/user/configs/registration.csv  
          • Semicolon-separated (“;”) – *including* the header line.  
      b.  /home/user/changes/firmware_versions.tsv  
          • Tab-separated (“\t”) – *including* the header line.

  2.  Required output directory tree (create it if it does not exist):
      • /home/user/reports  
      • /home/user/logs

  3.  Target report file that **must** be produced:
      /home/user/reports/config_change_matrix.tsv

      Exact required TSV layout (tab-separated, **one single tab between columns, Linux “\n” line-ending**):

      server_id	location	role	maint_window	version	release_date
      srv01	us-east	web	Sun 01:00	1.2.4	2024-01-21
      srv02	us-west	db	Mon 02:00	1.4.7	2024-02-10
      srv03	eu-central	cache	Tue 03:00	1.3.2	2024-02-28

      Notes for automated testing:
        • Column order and spelling must be exactly as shown above.
        • There must be **one** trailing newline at the end of the last line.
        • Use information exclusively from the two source files—do not “hard-code” values.

  4.  Verification log that **must** be produced:
      /home/user/logs/column_extraction.log

      Exact format (four lines, Linux “\n”, no extra whitespace):

      registration.csv lines: 4
      firmware_versions.tsv lines: 4
      config_change_matrix.tsv lines: 4
      build_status: SUCCESS

      (Each value is the literal decimal count of lines in the file, header included.)

  5.  Technical constraints / hints:
      • Solve the task only with standard GNU utilities that would be available in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu environment
        (e.g. cut, paste, mkdir, printf, cat, wc, etc.).
      • You do **not** have root privileges; stay completely within /home/user.
      • The log file is used by the automated grader—match the format *exactly*.
      • Feel free to use temporary intermediate files inside /home/user/tmp or /tmp if helpful, but they will not be checked.

  The session is interactive; issue whatever terminal commands you need so that,
  when you finish, the two files described above exist with precisely the
  content and formatting specified.
truth:
Initial files that the sandbox provides before the agent starts:

  1.  Path: /home/user/configs/registration.csv  
      Content (4 lines, LF line-endings):

      server_id;location;role;maint_window
      srv01;us-east;web;Sun 01:00
      srv02;us-west;db;Mon 02:00
      srv03;eu-central;cache;Tue 03:00

  2.  Path: /home/user/changes/firmware_versions.tsv  
      Content (4 lines, LF line-endings, **tab-separated**):

      server_id	version	release_date
      srv01	1.2.4	2024-01-21
      srv02	1.4.7	2024-02-10
      srv03	1.3.2	2024-02-28

  Expected artefacts after the agent completes:

  1.  /home/user/reports/config_change_matrix.tsv  
      (exactly six tab-separated fields per row, LF endings):

      server_id	location	role	maint_window	version	release_date
      srv01	us-east	web	Sun 01:00	1.2.4	2024-01-21
      srv02	us-west	db	Mon 02:00	1.4.7	2024-02-10
      srv03	eu-central	cache	Tue 03:00	1.3.2	2024-02-28

  2.  /home/user/logs/column_extraction.log  
      (four lines, LF endings):

      registration.csv lines: 4
      firmware_versions.tsv lines: 4
      config_change_matrix.tsv lines: 4
      build_status: SUCCESS
